summary: Recent updates to the WebRTC (the technology behind video calls) implementation in Firefox focus on enhancing logging, improving security, and refining media capabilities. The introduction of **MOZ_LOG_FMT** improves how logs are formatted, aiding developers in diagnosing issues more effectively. Additionally, updates to WebRTC's H.264 video codec include rejecting requests that exceed specified resolution or framerate limits, which helps ensure optimal video performance. Security has also been bolstered with the expiration update for webrtcdtls.KEA, ensuring that encryption keys remain secure over time. These changes collectively enhance both the reliability and efficiency of real-time communications in Firefox.
